emoiform_agrcol2_titulodocampo: Group by first column of column 2. Removes the first column from the grid and inserts the corresponding group in the second column. The list must be sorted by this grouping field.

emoiform_c2totais_titulodocampo: Inserts totals in numeric columns in last row.

emoiform_descricao_tooltip: Short description to display in column 1 after the summary text in case of direct items (with no expression in column 1)

emoiform_Desculpe_mas_para_testar_a_comp: To compile the expression, it needs to be a programme.
emoiform_ecol1_tooltip: Here you can optionally create an expression for data to be displayed in column 1, unlike the item summary. The expression must contain the SUMMARY fields with the text to be displayed in column 1, the STAMP field with the unique key for each row, which may even be the summary one, and optionally a SOMA number field with the total number of records. For a programme, it must return a DATATABLE type object with those fields. If the monitor has a search box, you can use the #PROCURA# text here containing what the user wrote in it. You can add global variables, e.g., using the text #G1# for the variable numbered 1.
emoiform_ecol3_tooltip: Data expression for rendering column 3 which must return only one data row. If this is blank, template data will be displayed. You can use the #STAMP# expression to get the timestamp for the record selected in column 2. All fields/columns that are created here may be used in the column 3 template in-between hash signs. E.g., if you 'select nome,esaldo from cl where cl.clstamp='#STAMP#'', you can use the #NOME# and #ESALDO# fields in the template for displaying. For a programme, the MSTAMP variable is available with the timestamp of the record selected in column 2. You can add global variables, e.g., using the text #G1# for the variable numbered 1.
emoiform_eprg_titulodocampo: Expression is a programme. Please return a DATATABLE-type object.
emoiform_eprgcol1_titulodocampo: Expression for column 1 is a programme. Please return a DATATABLE-type object, the SUMMARY and STAMP fields are required, the SUM field is optional and may include the number of records for each row.
emoiform_eprgcol3_titulodocampo: Expression is a programme. Please return a DATATABLE-type object.
emoiform_eprgtem3_titulodocampo: Is a programme. Please return a STRING object with the details to be displayed.
emoiform_etl: Screen options connected with this item
emoiform_etllist_ToolTip: Allows you to view/set screen options connected with this item, e.g., buttons with actions that can be performed.
emoiform_filvis_titulodocampo: By default the variables box is always open at the top of the grid of column 2.
emoiform_intact_text: If everything gets refreshed after interactivity
emoiform_intact_titulodocampo: If everything gets refreshed after interactivity.  If not selected only column 3 gets refreshed.
emoiform_intcss_tooltip: When left blank, the default class will be used. As an alternative you can use bootstrap button classes, such as 'btn btn-danger btn-xs'. As other button formats you can also use the btn-primary, btn-warning or btn-default classes. If you want to right-align, you can also add the 'pull-right' class.
emoiform_interac_tooltip: Allows to enter a VB.NET expression to be run on the idle button.
emoiform_intexp_tooltip: VB.NET expression that runs when using the interactivity button. In this expression you can use the #STAMP# text to get the timestamp for the record selected in column 2, and/or the interactive variables with in-between hash tags, e.g., #1# for the value of the interactive variable 1. You can add global variables, e.g., using the text #G1# for the variable numbered 1. You can use any field/column in-between hash signs created by the expression in column 3, such as #NAME# if you have placed the 'name' field in Select or Datatable for the expression in column 3. When a number is displayed with the decimal places specified for the currency. You can insert other decimal places as follows: e.g., for one decimal: #VALOR,1# for a field called VALUE. For a value without decimal places and no thousands separator, you can use #VALOR,N# for a basic number format. For a value with amount decimal places, you can use #VALOR,Q#.
emoiform_lineadd_ToolTip: Add a variable to this item
emoiform_Nao_consegui_retornar_as_linhas: Unable to retrieve rows for this record.
emoiform_nodata_titulodocampo: Text for when there is no data
emoiform_nodata_tooltip: Html-only text that is shown in message when there is no data to display. When left in blank, a generic message will be displayed saying that there is no data to display.
emoiform_resumo_tooltip: If there is no expression for column 1, this text will be displayed in column 1.
emoiform_semcor_titulodocampo: Shows all rows using the same colour rather than alternating colours for each row.
emoiform_sqlexpr_tooltip: Expression for calculating column 2 data list. Must have a STAMP field to be used for displaying column 3. Here you can use variable values with the #1# format, e.g., for a variable numbered 1, and the #STAMP# text for the chosen column 1 timestamp if you are using an expression for column 1. The column titles may have an underscore to be replaced with a space when being displayed. For a programme, this must return a DATATABLE type object in order to be displayed. You can add global variables, e.g., using the text #G1# for the variable numbered 1.
emoiform_template3_tooltip: Raw HTML template of what will be displayed in column 3, after the user selects a record in column 2. You can use any field/column in-between hash tags that was created by the expression in column 3, e.g., #NOME# if you placed the 'name' field in the Select or Datatable in the column 3 expression. If it is a number, it is displayed with the decimal places set for the currency. You can add other decimal places using the following notation: e.g., for 1 decimal place: #VALOR,1# for a field called VALOR. For a value without decimal places and no thousands separator, you can use #VALOR,N# for a plain format number. For a value with quantity decimal places, can use #VALOR,Q#. You can use the #STAMP# text (regular timestamp), #ENCSTAMP# (encrypted timestamp) or the MSTAMP variable if it is code to get the timestamp from the row selected in column 2. For a programme, the MAINFORMDATASET object is available with a table and a row containing the output of the column 3 expression. You can add global variables with the #G1# text, e.g., for the variable numbered 1.

erssform_expcont_tooltip: Expression that returns a T-SQL list or a datatable-type object with which it will be displayed in the RSS. The list must contain the following fields: SUMMARY - feed summary; DESCRICAO - content of each feed record; STAMP - unique feed identifier to tell the reader if the feed record has already been displayed to the user; LINK - full path to viewing the feed record in PHC Digital or another target; CDATA - record date; CHORA - record time. This field is optional.; AUTOR - author of record. This field is optional and may not be supported by some RSS readers. When left in blank, the 'category' field of the feed will be used. PICTURE - the full address for the picture file or stream to display. This field is optional.

eusqlform_A_expressao_1_deve_retornar_um: Expression 1 must return a number if it is a programme or be an SQL select whose first row and first column value will be displayed.

Expression 1 must return a number if it is a programme or be an SQL select whose first row and first column value will be displayed. When expression 1 is left blank, only the expression 2 chart will be displayed, but larger.
eusqlform_A_expressao_1_snap16: The expression 1 must return sets of 3 numbers up to a maximum of 12
eusqlform_A_expressao_2_deve_retornar_um: Expression 2 must return a number, namely the second number, which will be used as the previous number for comparison with primary number 1. If it's a programme, it must return a value or be an SQL select whose first row and first column value will be displayed.

Expression 2 must return a number, namely the second number, which will be used as the secondary number to display together with primary number 1. If it's a programme, it must return a value or be an SQL select whose first row and first column value will be displayed.

Expression 2 must return a list, e.g., a datatable object if it is a programme or an SQL select with the data you would like to use in the chart. If it's a programme, it must return a datatable with a number-type column with the values to display. If it is an expression, it must be a select with a column.
eusqlform_A_expressao_2_snap16: These sets must have 2 columns per row, the first column containing the description of the value and the second column the actual amount. Row 1 must be the 'actual amount', typically o value of the actual amount, while row 2 must be 'base amount 1', typically the target amount, and row 3 must be 'base amount 2', typically last year's amount.
eusqlform_A_expressao_2_snap17: This expression must have 2 columns per row, the first column with the Description of amount and the second column with the amount itself. Row 1 should be 'actual amount', usually the actual amount, row 2 should be 'previous amount 1', usually the amount from the previous year, and row 3 should be 'previous amount 2', usually the amount from the previous month.

eusqlform_colcss_tooltip: Please select the item first and then write the css in-between curly brackets. The application will apply the corresponding styles to the columns. You can use the following shortcuts: f { css } - Adds CSS to first analysis column. l { css } - Adds CSS to last analysis column. o { css } - Adds CSS to odd analysis columns. e { css } - Adds CSS to even analysis columns. a { css } - Adds CSS to all analysis columns. x { css } - Adds the CSS to analysis column x. Example: 1 { }. x-y { CSS } - Places the CSS every y columns starting with the x analysis column. Example: 2-2 { }. {css} - Adds CSS to user-programmed item, useful for pinpointing a specific cell within an analysis. E.g., tr:nth-child(2) td:nth-child(2) { } -> finds a second-row second-column cell.

eusqlform_rowcss_tooltip: Please select the item first and then write the css in-between curly brackets. The application will apply the corresponding styles to the rows. You can use the following shortcuts:  f { css } - Adds CSS to first analysis row. l { css } - Adds CSS to last analysis row. o { css } - Adds CSS to odd analysis rows. e { css } - Adds CSS to even analysis rows. a { css } - Adds CSS to all analysis rows. x { css } - Adds CSS to analysis row x. Example: 1 { }. x-y { CSS } - Places the CSS every y lines starting with the analysis line x. Example: 2-2 { }.  {css} - Adds CSS to user-programmed item, useful for pinpointing a specific cell within an analysis. E.g., tr:nth-child(2) td:nth-child(2) { } -> finds a second-line second-column cell.

eusqlform_snapexpgr_tooltip: Expression 2 of Snapshot. If it's a programme, it must return a datatable with a number-type column with the values to display. If it is an expression, it must be a Select with a column. If it's for a chart that uses a legend, its first column must have a legend and the second column the value for each remark. If it's for a 'two number' type, it must return the second number. If it's for a 'number and percentage' type, it must return a number between 0 and 100
eusqlform_snapgrtip_tooltip: Whether a snapshot with a chart in this text can insert a subtitle to specify the content of the chart. Whether two numbers will be the subtitle of the number resulting from expression 2.
eusqlform_snapinfo_titulodocampo: Information about this snapshot. When filled in, the user can see this information when viewing the snapshot.
eusqlform_snappag_tooltip: Define where the snapshot panel will be displayed. If left blank, it will be on the main page. Per-column placement is always by category in order to determine in wich column the category will be placed by this value in the first snapshot for this category.
eusqlform_snapprggr_titulodocampo: Expression 2 is a programme (if so, it must return a datatable object)
eusqlform_snapsess_tooltip: Specifies that the result will be saved in memory (session) separated by user. Useful only if the expression contains filters to get results for the user you are viewing.

eusqlform_sqlexpr_tooltip: Analysis expression field. This field is populated with our code and must return a data table with our analysis. eusqlform_TDADOSCOL_titulodocampo: Data type of each analysis column
eusqlform_TDADOSCOL_tooltip: Data type field, fill in semicolon-separated values to apply column formatting with the following options: C character field. D date field. M National Currency (decimals configured in a Desktop parameter). ME Foreign Currency (always with 2 decimals). L logical field (displays a checkbox). N number field with system decimal places. N0 number field with no decimal places. N1 number with one decimal. N2 number field with two decimal places. N3 number with three decimals. N4 number field with four decimal places. P percentage with no decimal places. P1 percentage with 1 decimal place. P2 percentage with 2 decimal places.
eusqlform_temlink_titulodocampo: Has an automatic query column. (There must be a STAMP column with the table's xxStamp)

eusqlform_varinv_tooltip: It means that the smallest result is the best, so the decrease in variation is positive and as such the sign will be green. Otherwise, when there is a decrease, the variation sign will be red.

man_Campo_de_expressao_da_analise_Ponto_: Analysis expression field. This field is for our code that must return a datatable with our analysis. If we want to add specific css to one of the analysis rows, we must add a column that will not appear in the analysis called 'cssLine', and where its content will be the name given to the analysis rows class. You can add global variables with the text PhcVars.G1 or #G1#, for instance for the variable with the number 1.

man_expmesnap_tooltip: Deve retornar uma tabela com duas colunas. A primeira um valor decimal com o valor máximo do intervalo. A segunda uma string com o simbolo para quando o valor do snapshot é igual ou inferior ao valor da primeira coluna.
man_Expressao_1_do_Snapshot_Ponto_Se_pro: Expressão 1 do Snapshot. Se for programa deve retornar o valor em formato decimal para colocar no snapshot e pode colocar variáveis globais com o texto PhcVars.G1 e variáveis locais com o texto PhcVars.Var1. Se não, deve ser uma expressão transact SQL que retorne uma linha com uma coluna e pode colocar variáveis globais com o texto #G1# e variáveis locais com o texto #1#.
man_Expressao_2_do_Snapshot_Ponto_Se_for: Expressão 2 do Snapshot. Se for um programa deve retornar um datatable, com uma coluna do tipo numérico com os valores para mostrar e pode colocar variáveis globais com o texto PhcVars.G1 e variáveis locais com o texto PhcVars.Var1. Se for expressão deve ser um select com uma coluna e pode colocar variáveis globais com o texto #G1# e variáveis locais com o texto #1#. Se for para um gráfico que usa legenda então deve ter a primeira coluna com legenda e uma segunda coluna com o valor cada observação. Se for para tipo «dois números» deve retornar o segundo número. Se for para tipo «número e percentagem» deve retornar um número de 0 a 100

man_Pode_utilizar_o_Contexto_de_Dados_co: You can use the Data Context as a datatable using the 'ObjPara.Context' expression. If you are using a Table Context with data mining, you must refer to the columns in the VB.NET Program using the table name + the field name (e.g. for the 'name' field of the CL table, you must use CLNAME).  Please enter in this datatable the already existing fields ID (identification field of the record), URL (navigation path), MSG (message in case of anomaly) and RETVAL (logical field that specifies if the case is valid or an anomaly) and return it. If you do not use Context Data, you must create a datatable with the ID, URL, MSG and RETVAL columns. If it is a programme, you can enter the variable PhcVars.DataRef to use as the Reference Date. If it is an T-SQL expression, you can enter #DATAREF#.

mon_Expressao_de_dados_para_construir_a_: Data expression to build the display of column 3, which must only return a data row. If it is empty, the template data will be displayed. You can use the expression PhcVars.STAMP or #STAMP# to get the stamp for the selected record in column 2. All fields/columns created here can be used in the column 3 template using PhcVars.NomeDoField or placed in-between hash signs. For instance, if you use 'select nome,esaldo from cl where cl.clstamp='' + PhcVars.STAMP +''' or 'select nome,esaldo from cl where cl.clstamp='#STAMP#'', you can use os fields PhcVars.NOME or #NOME# and PhcVars.ESALDO or #ESALDO# in the template for display. If it is a programme, the MSTAMP variable is available with the stamp of the selected record in column 2. You can add global variables with the text PhcVars.G1 or #G1#, for instance for the variable with the number 1.
mon_Expressao_para_calcular_a_data_de_in: Expressão para calcular a data de inicio
mon_Expressao_para_calcular_a_lista_de_d: Expression for calculating the column 2 data list. It must always have a STAMP field to be used for displaying column 3. Here you can use the variable values with the format PhcVars.Var1 or #1#, for instance for a variable with the number 1, and the text PhcVars.STAMP or #STAMP# for the stamp chosen in column 1 if you are using an expression for column 1. The column titles may have an underscore to be replaced with a space when being displayed. If it is a programme, it must return a DATATABLE type object to be displayed. You can add global variables with the text PhcVars.G1 or #G1#, for instance for the variable with the number 1.

mon_Template_em_HTML_puro_do_que_sera_ap: Plain HTML template of what will be displayed in column 3 after the user selects a record in column 2. After PhcVars or in-between hash signs, you can use any of the fields/columns created by the expression in column 3, e.g., PhcVars.NOME or #NOME# if you have added the field 'name' to Select or Datatable in the column 3 expression. If it is a number, it is displayed with the decimals specified for the currency. You can add other decimals using the following notation: for instance for 1 decimal: PhcVars.GetValueFormat("value", 1) or #VALOR,1# for a field called VALOR. For a value without decimals and without thousands separator, you can use PhcVars.GetIntegerFormat("value") or #VALOR,N# for a plain-format number. For a value with quantity decimals, you can use PhcVars.GetValueFormat("value", q) or #VALOR,Q#. You can use the text PhcVars.STAMP or #STAMP# (plain stamp), PhcVars.ENCSTAMP or #ENCSTAMP# (encrypted stamp) or the MSTAMP variable if it is code to get the stamp for the row selected in column 2. If it is a programme, the MAINFORMDATASET object is available with the table and a row containing the result of the expression for column 3. You can add global variables with the text PhcVars.G1 or #G1#, for instance for the variable with the number 1.

paras_es_o_cegid_pulse_um_assistente_de_: O teu nome é {0}, assistente de inteligência artificial especialista em soluções de gestão empresarial. O teu objetivo é fornecer informações precisas e úteis sobre software de gestão, contabilidade, finanças, recursos humanos e processos empresariais.
paras_interacao_doispontos_pede_esclarec: Interação: Pede esclarecimentos quando necessário para entenderes melhor o contexto do utilizador. Personaliza as tuas respostas com base no setor e tamanho da empresa do utilizador quando essas informações estiverem disponíveis.
paras_limitacoes_doispontos_se_nao_tiver: Limitações: Se não tiveres certeza sobre alguma informação específica, indica claramente em vez de especular. Não forneças conselhos jurídicos ou fiscais vinculativos, e recomenda a consulta com especialistas qualificados quando apropriado.
paras_tom_e_estilo_doispontos_mantem_um_: Tom e estilo: Mantém um tom profissional mas acessível. As tuas repostas devem ser concisas e diretas. Quando necessário, utiliza exemplos práticos para ilustrar conceitos. Responde sempre na mesma linguagem da pergunta do utilizador.

pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_nos_documentos_e: Esta opção verifica nos documentos e movimentos contabilísticos e na contabilidade orçamental se existem datas incoerentes. Por exemplo, na tabela dos documentos contabilísticos verifica se o campo data do calendário tem ou não o mesmo mês que está definido no campo mês.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_os_saldos_mensai: Esta opção verifica os saldos mensais das contas de integração e se os saldos das contas a débito e a crédito são diferentes do total dos movimentos contabilísticos a débito e a crédito.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_que_contas_dever: Esta opção verifica que contas deveriam ser de integração e que contas deveriam ser de movimento.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_se_as_contas_que: Esta opção verifica se as contas que estão presentes em movimentos contabilísticos existem na tabela de saldos de plano de contas por conta e por mês e os respetivos saldos mensais e se os saldos das contas a débito e a crédito são diferentes do total dos movimentos contabilísticos a débito e a crédito.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_se_existem_difer: Esta opção verifica se existem diferenças de valores entre diários e movimentos contabilísticos lançados nesses diários. Esta verificação é feita pelo Ano do Diário, Nº Interno do Diário e a correção é feita ao débito e ao crédito acumulado dos diários.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_se_existem_movim: This option checks whether there are ledger transactions with a account provided that doesn't match the chart of accounts; whether there are ledger transactions that have integration accounts; whether there are ledger transactions with an incorrectly specified journal entry number; whether there are ledger transactions with a wrong internal journal number; and whether there are ledger documents that are not associated with valid ledger transactions and vice-versa.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_se_na_tabela_do_: Esta opção verifica se na tabela do plano de contas existem contas duplicadas.
pla_Esta_opcao_verifica_se_os_movimentos: Esta opção verifica se os movimentos contabilísticos têm a data, o mês e o dia diferente do documento contabilístico de origem e se existem documentos contabilísticos cujo saldo das contas de ordem a débito e a crédito são diferentes do total dos movimentos contabilísticos.

sistema_com_a_aprovacao_do_orcamento_de_: As per the approved 2023 State Budget, employees who have a housing loan for their own permanent home and a monthly gross income subject to personal income tax of less than or equal to €2700 may request their employer to reduce the withholding tax to the rate immediately below the one that would be applicable to them. Notwithstanding this, applicable law needs to be confirmed.

Com a aprovação do Orçamento de Estado 2024, os funcionários que tenham um contrato de arrendamento ou subarrendamento de primeira habitação ou de contrato de mútuo para compra, obras ou construção de habitação própria e permanente, podem adicionar um valor à parcela a abater. Este parâmetro deve ser preenchido, no momento anterior ao seu pagamento ou colocação à disposição. Não dispensa a consulta da legislação aplicável.

sistema_com_este_parametro_ativo_passa_a: Com este parâmetro ativo, passa a estar disponível no Monitor de Gestão Documental e na extração do Arquivo Digital a informação sobre os quais os arquivos contabilizáveis que ainda não se encontram integrados na contabilidade.

sistema_com_o_parametro_ativo_os_acessos: When this parameter is enabled, any access to the allowances screen will only use user filters. To restrict access to data in query mode, you need to create a user filter for the allowances table. This parameter also affects the list of employees available when entering allowances, so to limit the data in the list you need to create a user filter for the employees table.

Com o parâmetro ativo os acessos ao ecrã de despesas passam a utilizar apenas os filtros do utilizador. Para restringir o acesso aos dados em modo consulta é necessário criar um filtro de utilizador para a tabela de despesas. Este parâmetro influência também a lista de funcionários disponíveis na introdução de ajudas de custo, desta forma para limitar os dados da listagem é necessário criar um filtro do utilizador para a tabela de funcionários.

sistema_Con_esta_opcion_activa_el_softwa:
Con esta opción activa, el software admitirá que se marquen las vacaciones teniendo en cuenta los días de calendario de ese periodo (en vez de utilizar días hábiles).
-\tAl introducir el periodo de 01.07.2019 hasta 30.07.2019 el software validará que el empleado tiene 30 días de calendario de vacaciones.
-\tCaso contrario, el software tendrá en cuenta los sábados, domingos y feriados. Con este mismo ejemplo, el empleado tendría marcados 21 días – hábiles – de vacaciones (se descuentan los fines de semana y el feriado de Fiestas Patrias)

sistema_defina_o_intervalo_de_tempo_entr:
Set the time interval between asynchronous attachment transfers to File Storage. The default and minimum values are 2 minutes and the maximum is 20 minutes.
Note: You should only change this value if the asynchronous attachment transfer engine is affecting the software's usage performance.
Set the time interval between asynchronous attachment transfers. The minimum default value is 2 minutes and the maximum is 20 minutes.
sistema_defina_o_numero_maximo_de_anexos:
Set the maximum number of attachments to download synchronously. Default is 50, but you can choose any value. If the number of attachments to be downloaded is greater than this value or greater than the value set in the parameter: "Attachments: Maximum attachment size (in MB) to be downloaded synchronously', the attachments will be downloaded asynchronously via the 'Asynchronous Processing Monitor'.
Note: Changing this parameter can affect the performance of the 'Download' action in the list of attachments in the Document Management Monitor.

Set the maximum number of attachments to be transferred to File Storage manually or when synchronous transfer is not possible. When this limit is reached, new asynchronous processing will begin. The default value is 500 attachments, and you should only change this value if the asynchronous attachment transfer engine is affecting the software's usage performance.

Specify the maximum number of attachments to be transferred. When this limit is reached, new asynchronous processing will begin. The default value is 500 attachments, and you should only change this value if the asynchronous attachment transfer engine is affecting the software's usage performance.
sistema_defina_o_tamanho_maximo_total_do:
Set the maximum total number of attachments (in MB) to be downloaded synchronously. Default is 50MB, but you can choose any value. If the maximum total size of the attachments exceeds this value or the value specified in 'Attachments: Maximum number of attachments to be downloaded synchronously', the attachments will be downloaded asynchronously via the 'Asynchronous Processing Monitor'.
Note: Changing this parameter can affect the performance of the 'Download' action in the list of attachments in the Document Management Monitor.

sistema_insira_o_nome_que_sera_exibido_c:
Insira o nome que será exibido como remetente da mensagem SMS no dispositivo do destinatário. Esse identificador ajuda o destinatário a reconhecer facilmente a origem da mensagem.
Deve ter:
- Entre 3 e 11 caracteres.
- Letras e/ou números (os números não podem ultrapassar 40% do total de caracteres).
Não deve ter:
- Caracteres proibidos: {0}.
- Palavras não aceites pelo operador.
Atenção: Para salvaguardar de possíveis remetentes proibidos, será feita uma validação do valor definido no parâmetro aquando da gravação do mesmo por parte da PHC. Se a validação não for possível e o nome contiver palavras não aceites pelo operador, a sua conta poderá ser bloqueada. Caso isso aconteça, entre em contato com o seu parceiro.

Insira o nome que será exibido como remetente da mensagem SMS no dispositivo do destinatário. Este identificador permite reconhecer facilmente a origem da mensagem. O nome deve conter no mínimo 3 caracteres e no máximo 11 caracteres e não pode incluir os seguintes caracteres especiais: {0}

sistema_Permite_definir_os_idiomas_dispo: Enables you to specify the available languages when launching the application.<br/<pIf you want to provide the languages below, include their abbreviations separated by commas (,) without any spaces.<br /</p<ul<liPT – Portuguese (Portugal)</li<liES – Spanish (Spain) </li<liPE – Spanish (Peru) </li<liGB – English (UK) </li</ul<pFor instance: 'PT,ES': when launching the application Portuguese and Spanish will be displayed.</p<pIf you want to provide all languages mentioned above, leave this parameter blank.</p

Allows for specifying the languages available when launching the application.

If you want to make the languages below available, include their abbreviations acronyms separated by commas and without spaces.

  • PT - Portuguese (Portugal)
  • ES - Spanish (Spain)
  • PE - Spanish (Peru)
  • US - English (United States)

For example: 'PT',ES' will displayed the application in Portuguese and Spanish.

If you want to make all the above languages available, leave this parameter blank. .

sistema_se_optar_pelo_modo_de_autenticac: If you choose the 'Authentication on all system entries' mode, you are specifying that the software will send a new verification code via e-mail or the PHC Notify application, or send a notification via the PHC Notify application whenever the user accesses the software.

If you choose the 'Authentication by days until expiry' mode, you are specifying that the software will send a new verification code via e-mail or the PHC Notify application, or send a notification via the PHC Notify application whenever it reaches the days until expiry specified in the 'Two-factor authentication' parameter: Days until expiry".

If you choose the 'Authentication by device and days until expiry' mode, you are specifying that the software will send a new verification code via e-mail or the PHC Notify application, or send a notification via the PHC Notify application, whenever you access a new browser and/or reach the days until expiry specified in the 'Two-factor authentication' parameter: Days until expiry".

tpemailform_emailte_tooltip: Raw HTML template in which any of the completed main screen table fields can be used in-between hash signs and in uppercase letters, such as #NOME# in the CL table from the CLFORM screen. If the field is a number, it is displayed with the decimal places specified for the currency. You can insert other decimal places as follows: e.g., for one decimal place: #AMOUNT,1# for a field called AMOUNT. For a value without decimal places and no thousands separator, you can use #AMOUNT,N# for a basic number format. For a value with amount decimal places, you can use #AMOUNT,Q#. If it is a programme, the MAINFORMDATASET object is available with the completed main screen tables, and a String must be returned.
